commit.message:
Enable HTTP server request metrics for Prometheus/Grafana

Enable HTTP server observations and histogram distribution so that
http_server_requests_seconds_bucket and _count metrics are exposed
on the actuator/prometheus endpoint.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix post creation to use JSON request body instead of query params

Add @RequestBody to FeedController, send content as JSON body in load
test client, and configure Redis ObjectMapper with JavaTimeModule for
proper date serialization.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix neo4j container startup failure caused by read-only volume mount

Neo4j 5 chowns everything under /var/lib/neo4j/ on startup, which
fails on the :ro mounted init-graph.cypher. The file is already
mounted separately in the neo4j-init sidecar container.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Implement news feed system with fanout architecture

Spring Boot 3.4 / Java 21 app with Nginx load balancer, Post Service
(PostgreSQL + Redis cache), Fanout Service (RabbitMQ → workers → News Feed
Cache), Neo4j graph DB for friendships, Prometheus/Grafana monitoring,
load test client, unit tests, and Docker Compose orchestration.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: Chrome extension custom prompt now always generates a result

The popup worked because it had generateDemoPrompt() as a local fallback.
The content script's custom generate had NO fallback — if the API failed
(auth, network, any error), it just showed an error and did nothing.

Now: tries API first, falls back to local smart prompt generation.
Always inserts a quality prompt into ChatGPT, no matter what.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: Chrome extension recursive message loop breaking prompt generation

Background's handlePlayAudioLogo was sending PLAY_AUDIO_LOGO message back
to itself via chrome.runtime.sendMessage, causing infinite recursion that
crashed the service worker and killed all message handlers including
GENERATE_PROMPT. Renamed offscreen message to OFFSCREEN_PLAY_AUDIO.

commit.message:
Fix hero section left-alignment by adding flex-direction: column

The hero-section used display: flex with default flex-direction: row,
causing .container and .hero-scroll-indicator to sit side by side
horizontally. This squeezed the container width and pushed content left.
Adding flex-direction: column and justify-content: center ensures proper
vertical stacking and centering.

commit.message:
fix: 修复所有 ESLint 错误和警告

## 修复内容

### 删除未使用的导入（28 处）
- graph/page.tsx: CardContent
- kb/page.tsx: ChevronRight, ChevronDown, ImageIcon, Trash2, FolderOpen, X, Upload, Card, Input
- settings/page.tsx: Palette, useRouter
- teacher/page.tsx: Filter, BarChart3, Activity, Target, MessageSquare, GraduationCap, Award
- workspace/page.tsx: Calendar, Code, Play, Pause, RotateCcw, Maximize2, Minimize2, Tag, CardTitle, Separator, Input
- general-settings-panel.tsx: Switch
- json-import-panel.tsx: Download

### 删除未使用的变量（15 处）
- kb/page.tsx: expandedFolders, setExpandedFolders
- path/page.tsx: mIndex, tIndex 参数
- settings/page.tsx: router
- teacher/page.tsx: Student 类型
- workspace/page.tsx: setSessions, setShowThinking, knowledgePoints, setKnowledgePoints, setNotes, setResources, filteredSessions, KnowledgePoint 类型

### 修复类型错误（5 处）
- graph/page.tsx:
  - 定义 ForceGraphRef 接口
  - handleNodeClick/handleNodeHover 参数类型
  - nodeCanvasObject 参数类型
  - linkColor 参数类型

### 修复 React Hook 警告（1 处）
- kb/page.tsx: useEffect 依赖从 selectedDoc?.id 改为 selectedDoc

## 验证结果
✔ No ESLint warnings or errors

commit.message:
fix: cron_local apagava todos os crons e task virava cron

Bug 1 - cron_local delete-all: quando o usuario pedia "remova o cron X",
a logica defaultava para deletar TODOS os crons ativos. Agora:
- Tenta casar pelo nome do cron mencionado no texto
- So apaga todos se o usuario disser "todos"/"tudo"/"all" explicitamente
- Se nao achar o nome, retorna None e delega ao agente de IA

Bug 2 - task virava cron: o agente nao conhecia o protocolo [task] porque
montar_prompt() so mencionava [cron]. Agora as instrucoes do prompt
distinguem claramente [task] (unica vez, segundo plano) de [cron]
(recorrente/periodico), com regra explicita de nunca confundir os dois.

Sync: copiado ALMA.md atualizado para ~/.brazuclaw/ALMA.md (deploy)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Auto-create output folder as part of Setup Test Data (#36)

* Auto-create output folder as part of Setup Test Data

- Add createTestOutputFolder() to TestRunner.gs: creates (or reuses)
  a 'Email Campaign Test Output' Drive folder and saves its ID to Config
- Include folder creation as step 4 in setupTestDataUI(), updating
  progress toasts and confirmation dialog to reflect 4 steps
- Mock DriveApp.getFoldersByName and createFolder in jest.setup.js
- Add 3 tests covering: new folder, reuse existing, saves to Config

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Auto-populate account config fields during Setup Test Data

- Add setupTestAccountConfig() to TestRunner.gs: sets TEST_EMAIL and
  REPLY_TO_EMAIL from Session.getActiveUser().getEmail(), SENDER_NAME
  to 'Test'; skips fields already configured
- Include as step 1 in setupTestDataUI() (now 5 steps total)
- Mock global.Session in jest.setup.js
- Add 3 tests covering: returns email, sets all fields, skips existing

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix Session permission error and use account email as fallback

- Switch Session.getActiveUser() to Session.getEffectiveUser() in
  setupTestAccountConfig() — avoids 'insufficient permissions' error
- Add userinfo.email OAuth scope to appsscript.json as a safety net
- Use Session.getEffectiveUser().getEmail() as fallback in
  createTestRecipientsSheet() instead of hardcoded 'test@example.com'
- Update jest mocks and tests to reflect getEffectiveUser

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix Email ID column not being populated after campaign send

- Increase initial wait before Gmail search from 1s to 3s
- Add one retry with 5s additional wait for any messages not yet indexed
- Track found rowIndex values in populateEmailIds() to identify missing ones
- Apply a 10-second buffer to batchStartTime comparison to handle clock
  skew between GAS server and Gmail message timestamps

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add Email ID as a required column, not a lazily-created one

- Add 'Email ID' to ensureRequiredColumns() so it is created alongside
  Email Status, Doc ID, etc. when the sheet is first set up
- Add 'Email ID' header and empty values to createTestRecipientsSheet()
  so the column is always present from the start

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add diagnostic logging to populateEmailIds

Log search query, thread count, message subjects and dates, and match
results to help diagnose why email IDs are not being retrieved.
Check logs in Apps Script editor → Executions after sending campaign.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix subject matching: trim whitespace and fix default subject template

- Trim both sides before comparing msg.getSubject() === recipient.subject
  to handle Gmail stripping trailing spaces (e.g. 'Message from ')
- Change default EMAIL_SUBJECT from '{{Name}}' to '{{First Name}}' to
  match the actual column header used in recipient sheets

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add Sent at column and use per-recipient timestamp for Gmail search

- Append sent timestamp footer to each email body (visible to recipient)
- Record per-recipient sentAt in sendEmail return value
- Store sentAt in new 'Sent at' column via updateRecipientSentAt()
- populateEmailIds now uses per-recipient sentAt (not shared batchStartTime)
  for precise time filtering, eliminating subject-only matching fragility
- Add 'Sent at' to ensureRequiredColumns() and createTestRecipientsSheet()

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

commit.message:
fix: mitigate LLM hallucination on batch reaction approvals

Two-pronged fix for Haiku 4.5 skipping tool calls when processing
multiple thumbs-up reactions simultaneously:

1. Serialize reaction processing via threading.Lock so the LLM only
   sees one approval request at a time (zulip_channel.py)
2. Add workspace/AGENTS.md bootstrap file with explicit tool-call
   discipline rules, seeded to persistent volume on first boot
   (Dockerfile.fly, start.sh)

Also strengthens the reaction content message to require tool results
and increases reaction timeout from 30s to 120s.

commit.message:
C-1: costiTot() NaN guard — Number(v)||0 in reduce (v18.94)

Riga 299: Object.values(u.costi).reduce((s,v) => s+(Number(v)||0), 0)
Riga 303: stessa protezione in budgetCostiTot

Se un valore in u.costi è undefined o stringa, il precedente s+v
propagava NaN silenziosamente su tutta la Plancia. Con Number()||0
il valore malformato è trattato come 0 e il calcolo prosegue.
Zero impatto su dati corretti. TC 2025/2026/2027: 0 errori.

commit.message:
Add ds:draft-email command (#4)

* Add ds:draft-email command for partner email drafting

Implements the draft-email command that:
- Pulls ticket context from Front conversations or free-text input
- Detects escalation signals (frienemies communication) before drafting
- Applies boundary doctrine gates to every question
- Researches answers via Thanx Docs and Keystone MCP
- Drafts structured emails following Dev Support communication standards
- Runs post-draft review checklist for accuracy and compliance
- Supports tone overrides (friendly/neutral/firm/executive)

Sources: Notion Tech Ops Playbooks (Email Review + Escalation),
Second Brain playbooks (email-drafting, communication-style, boundary-doctrine)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Address CodeRabbit review feedback on draft-email command

Fixes:
- Align default tone to "neutral" consistently (usage docs + drafting rules)
- Implement --internal flag with full Slack/Jira internal message format
  (separate template, skip partner salutation/signoff, no Front send guidance)
- Replace brittle single-argument CLI send example with file-based and
  stdin-based patterns for multiline email content

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix review issues: version bump, correct MCP tool names, add fallbacks

- Bump package.json 0.1.0 -> 0.2.0 (new command = minor bump)
- Fix Front MCP tool name: front_list_conversation_messages -> front_list_messages
  (matches triage.md and actual Keystone MCP interface)
- Fix Front script CLI example: replace fabricated --message-file/--stdin flags
  with actual interface (--draft CNV_ID "message" and --send CNV_ID "message")
- Add fallback for Front MCP unavailability: command degrades to free-text input
- Add fallback for research tools unavailability: mark claims as unverified,
  list in post-draft review for manual verification

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Remove Front API references from draft-email command

The command is draft-only — it presents text for the user to copy and
send manually. All Front interactions (drafts, sends) happen outside
the command. This keeps the command read-only and avoids coupling to
the Front API script.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Address PR review feedback: untrusted input, version sync, error fallbacks

- Add untrusted input guidance for partner message content (security defense-in-depth)
- Bump plugin.json version to 0.2.0 to match package.json
- Expand Front MCP fallback to cover auth/permission/not-found errors
- Add partial-proceed path for escalation scenarios
- Replace hardcoded name with role-based "your engineering lead"
- Add PII handling note for draft output
- Add explicit conditional branching in Step 7 output

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix JSON indentation and regenerate package-lock.json

Restore 2-space indent on version lines in package.json and plugin.json.
Regenerate package-lock.json to sync version from 0.2.0 to 0.4.0.

commit.message:
Refactor Getting Started notebook for beginner simplicity

Major changes:
- Replace abstract function-first approach with teach-then-abstract:
  walk through diagnoses step-by-step (load → describe → count → merge
  → wordcloud), then wrap into reusable function for remaining 4 types
- Remove all advanced Python patterns: no defaultdict, no heapq,
  no lambda. Use regular dicts, sorted(), and explicit loops
- Simplify function from 3 functions (get_basic_summary,
  generate_wordcloud, generate_complete_summary) down to 1 clear
  function (summarize_ehr_table) with intuitive parameter names
- Replace opaque code_cols dict pattern with simple code_col and
  optional code_version_col parameters
- Add explanations for string describe() output, d_ prefix convention,
  ! shell escape, why batch processing is needed
- Expand thin interpretation markdowns with concrete observations

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix compression guard: allow LLM compression with 2+ steps

The previous guard (len(steps) <= 3) prevented compression from ever
firing in short trajectories because the early-answer heuristic
produces at most 3-4 steps. LLM compression can handle any trajectory
length, so lower the guard to 2+ steps. Mechanical fallback still
requires 4+ steps.

Tested end-to-end: compression triggers → summary in tokenized
sequence → not masked as tool output → OAPL gradient flows through.

fix: mobile image upload — raise bodyLimit to 10MB and improve error reporting

Root cause: Fastify default bodyLimit was 1MB, rejecting mobile photos (3-8MB).

- Set global bodyLimit to 10MB in Fastify constructor
- Add application/octet-stream parser fallback for empty Content-Type

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

tests: Add test coverage for auto_mix_step safety guards

Adds tests/test_automix.py covering the two safety-critical guards in
auto_mix_step: runaway protection (consecutive-raise cap) and stale-input
detection. Also covers inactive channel skip and fader ceiling enforcement.

Key fix vs original spec: the runaway test uses slowly-decreasing input
(band > STALE_INPUT_BAND_DB) so the stale guard doesn't fire first, allowing
the runaway counter to accumulate past MAX_CONSECUTIVE_RAISES.
